text.title: Founder As Pm Survival Kit Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Founder As Pm Survival Kit must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Keep exactly 3 PM artefacts: a weekly cash-and-capacity dashboard, a 1-page client-status doc per active engagement, and a kill-list of stuck work. All other PMBoK artefacts are explicitly DROPPED.
rule.rationale: the founder bottleneck is attention, not documentation.

rule.statement: Time-box weekly PM work to 90 minutes. Anything that requires more is automation-debt or hire-debt.
rule.rationale: Mike Michalowicz, Profit First framing extended to attention.

rule.statement: Forbid any artefact that needs a stakeholder to read it. Founders run a closed loop; artefacts are for the founder's own future-self.

rationale.source: Single-user methodology principle: do not optimize for an audience that doesn't

rule.rationale: Single-user methodology principle: do not optimize for an audience that doesn't exist.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Run 2–3 person team comms without a full-time PM Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
